summary: A research team from the University of Science and Technology of China has achieved “the world’s first demonstration of a scalable building block for a quantum repeater,” according to the institution, marking a major advance in scalable quantum networks. Their findings, published in both Nature and Science, include development of a long-lived trapped-ion quantum memory, a highly efficient ion-photon interface, and a high-fidelity protocol. These enabled quantum entanglement to persist well beyond the time to connect segments, overcoming a core obstacle for long-distance quantum networks. The team also achieved high-fidelity entanglement between distant rubidium atoms and demonstrated device-independent quantum key distribution over 11 kilometers of fiber, extending previous results 3,000 times and confirming secure key generation up to 100 kilometers.
